message:
fix(fr-runtime): AUTH 2-arg respects nopass user even without requirepass [bd-frankenredis-authnopass]

The 5qln fix (AUTH 2-arg returns WRONGPASS for unknown users)
over-corrected: when authenticate_user short-circuits on
!auth_required(), the 2-arg path translated NotConfigured to
WRONGPASS regardless of whether the named user actually exists.

This broke 'AUTH default bad' on a freshly-started server with no
requirepass — upstream accepts it (default has nopass + any
password) but fr was rejecting it.

Introduce authenticate_user_by_acl which looks up the user in the
ACL and runs check_password() independently of auth_required().
The 2-arg AUTH path now uses this directly so:
  - existing-user-with-nopass + any password → OK
  - unknown user → WRONGPASS
  - existing user + wrong password → WRONGPASS

The 1-arg path keeps the upstream NotConfigured semantics when
!auth_required().

Verified byte-for-byte against vendored redis 7.2.4:
  AUTH default bad   → OK on both
  AUTH baduser bad   → WRONGPASS on both
  AUTH bad           → NotConfigured on both

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
